Your Request fbr Advice

Re:
Dear Mr. Owens:
This letter resggnd; lo IgYtfquest for advice regarding the campaign provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").r Please note that our advice is based .ol.iy on tt e Act. We
therejbre o.ffer no opirti,rt on the oppliccttion, if cuty, of other lctws.
QUESTTON
May a committee for election of a candidate for state office use the names, acldresses and
contribution data of donors to a candidate fbr federal office for the pllfpose of mailing campaign
materials to these donors if the information is obtained from a publicly available website.
CONCLUSION
The Act does not prohibit sttch a mailing. However, if over two hundred substantially
similar pieces of mail are sent within a calendaimonth, the mailing constitutes a ..mass mailing,,
under the Act and certain requirements mLlst be met, inclLrding a requirement that certain
information must appear on the outside of each piece of mail and on at least one of the inserts.
FACTS
You are the campaign manager for the committee "Alfonso Faustino for Assembly.2 you
wotrld like to mail campaign materials urging support for Mr. Fausinto's canclidacy, including a
' The Political Rettlrm Act is contained in GovernmentCode Sections lll000 through 9l0l.l. All statutory
ref'erencesaretotheGovernmentCode,unlessotherwiseindicated. TheregulationsoftheFairpolitical practices
Commission are contained in Sections I 8 I l0 through lti997 of Title 2 ol the Calitirrnia Code of Re_eulations. All
regulatory ret'erences are to 'ritle 2, Division 6 of thi California Code of Re-culations. unless other,,vise indicated.
' A person or c<tmbination of pers<lns beconres a committee upon (i) receiving contribr.rtions totaling
$l'(xr) or more in a calendar year' (ii)making independent expenditures totaling $r.(x)i) or mole in a calendar year,


solicitation for contributions. You intend to obtain information needed for this mailing from a
Iist of donors to another campaign appearing on a website, rvww.firndracc.huffingtonpi-rst.com.
The website was created for use in a federal presidential race. In your mailing, you
intend to state that you have leamed of the recipient;s contribution to pasi candidates from an
internet posting. You also intend to include a fundraising letter, your Lusiness card, a small
brochure and a ftrndraising envelope.
ANALYSIS
Nothing in the Act prohibits the use of any particular source for obtaining information to be used
for election related activity. The Act, and. regulaiions adopted under the Act, govems, among other
things, the reporting of contributions received and expendit.,r", made by a coimittee, the establishment
of bank accounts, recordkeeping, loan limits, treatment of outstanding debts, restrictions on
contributions to other candidates and committees, limitations on the ictivities of lobbyists and the
content of written solicitations for contributions.
Your inquiry implicates the mass mailing3 provisions of the Act. Section 84305 requires, among
other things, that a mass mailing contain the name, street address and city of the candidate or committee
on the outside of each piece of mail and on at least one side of the inserts included within each of the
mailings' The disclosures must be in no less than 6-point type and in a color or print that contrasts with
the background, so as to be easily legible. Regulations tS4:S and IB4ZL.2 set forth additional
requirements. We urge you to familiarize yourself with the mass mailing requirements of the Act and
related regulations.a
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
By: Valentina Joyce
Counsel, Legal Division
